briefdescription.para: Compute peak_lag, peak_mag, noise_est, test_stat from out_buf.

detaileddescription.para: Fills the four result fields in state from the correlation output already stored in state->out_buf (n complex samples). Call immediately after a dump (corr_execute returned n).

memberdef.argsstring: (const float complex *ref, size_t n, size_t dwell, size_t noise_lo, size_t noise_hi, det_noise_mode_t noise_mode, float threshold, int nthreads)
memberdef.name: detector_create

detaileddescription.para: Allocates a corr_state_t, a dp_f32_t ring buffer of capacity next_pow2(max(n, 512)), and all scratch buffers. The ring buffer satisfies the dp_f32_create() page-alignment constraint automatically.

parameterdescription.para: Reference signal, CF32, length n. May be freed after this call returns.
Frame / FFT length in complex samples. Must be >= 1.
Test-stat threshold. 0.0 = always emit a detection.
Passed through to corr_create(); currently ignored.

simplesect.para: Heap-allocated state, or NULL on allocation failure.

briefdescription.para: Destroy and free a detector instance.
Reset the correlator, ring buffer, and last-corr flag.

detaileddescription.para: Discards any partial frame buffered in the ring. Equivalent to starting fresh from the same reference.

briefdescription.para: Replace the reference signal and recompute conj(FFT(ref)).

detaileddescription.para: Also resets (see detector_reset()). The new reference must have the same length n that was passed to detector_create().

briefdescription.para: Change the threshold without rebuilding the object.

memberdef.argsstring: (detector_state_t *state, const float complex *in, size_t n_in, det_result_t *result, size_t max_results)
memberdef.name: detector_push

briefdescription.para: Push an arbitrary-length CF32 chunk through the detector.

detaileddescription.para: Writes n_in complex samples into the ring buffer in the minimum number of chunks that fit, then drains all complete n-sample frames through the correlator. On every int-dump a test statistic is computed; if it passes the threshold, a det_result_t is appended to result[]. The function returns as soon as n_in samples have been consumed or max_results detections have been stored, whichever comes first.
The result array must be pre-allocated by the caller. A stack array of 64 elements is sufficient for any realistic push size: det_result_tbuf[64]; size_tn=detector_push(det,chunk,len,buf,64);

parameterdescription.para: Input CF32 array of length n_in.
Number of complex samples to push.
Output array; caller allocates at least max_results.
Maximum detections to store; prevents unbounded output.

simplesect.para: Number of detections stored in result[].

detaileddescription.para: The core data path is: push(x[M]) → ring buffer → non-blocking drain → corr_execute (FFT correlator + int-dump) → |·|² + argmax → noise estimate → threshold gate → det_result_t[]

The factor 512 ensures the double-mapping page-alignment constraint is met: 512 samples × 8 bytes/sample (float complex) = 4096 bytes = 1 page. Any power-of-2 multiple of 512 also satisfies the constraint.
Noise estimation scratch buffer: Allocated at create time with capacity (noise_hi - noise_lo + 1) floats. Used only for DET_NOISE_MEDIAN to avoid a heap allocation on every push.
